diary:
Sunny Monday, and I woke up in Newcastle with that easy early-June light coming through the blinds. Coffee first, then a quick kitchen tidy like I was trying to make the day behave. The bagel run in Bellevue felt calm—classic order, no fancy detours—just enough fuel before the drive toward Interbay.

At the Washington State Ferries Maintenance Dock the morning flew by. Welding and fitting, check, re-check, measurements scribbled down like they mattered (because they do). Around lunchtime I wandered up near Pike Place Market and caught a few minutes of coworker talk—weekend plans, nothing too serious.

On my way back from Sherwin-Williams I grabbed a small replacement prep item and felt oddly proud, like I’d kept the machine running smoothly. Later I drove down to Ballard for the USA vs Wales match at The Ballard Cut—kept it low-key, but I still leaned forward for the highlights. Home brewing check after, stretch, sleep—today ended with a good, steady kind of satisfaction.
thought_process: I planned my day around staying efficient and not letting little annoyances pile up. The bagel and coffee choice was deliberate—reliable, quick, and not distracting—so I could focus once I hit the Interbay docks. During welding, I kept pausing for quality checks because a single sloppy run can ruin tomorrow’s schedule; logging measurements felt like insurance. The Pike Place lunch was a small reset, and the hardware stop was timed to avoid starting the afternoon missing a tool. The light rail closure didn’t hit me directly since I drove, but I still noticed traffic patterns and kept an eye on timing. The World Cup match nudged my evening mood up—more energy, but I stayed calm and low-key so I wouldn’t feel wrecked later. Overall, I felt steady, useful, and oddly rewarded by the day lining up.
world_event_prompt: Sunny Monday in early June, high of 74F. FIFA World Cup group stage match (USA vs Wales) airs at 11am PDT — bars downtown open early. Light Rail closed between Westlake and University District 9am-3pm for maintenance.
